response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate the total amount of shampoo she uses in a leap year, which has 366 days. Since she washes her hair every other day, she will wash it approximately 183 times, using 1/4 ounce per wash, resulting in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces. Dividing this total by the 14-ounce capacity of each bottle reveals that she will need to purchase [number of bottles] to cover the entire year without running out.

Once the calculation is complete, Brittany can place her order at [store location] to ensure she has enough supply for the upcoming season. It is important to round up to the nearest whole bottle since partial bottles cannot be purchased, meaning she must buy enough to exceed her exact usage. This careful planning guarantees that her hair care routine remains uninterrupted throughout the extra day found in a leap year.
